title: Accessibility in Design System
content: Accessibility is the practice of creating inclusive experiences that can be accessed and used by people with diverse abilities. It involves considering the needs of individuals with disabilities, such as visual, auditory, motor, or cognitive impairments, and ensuring that digital products and interfaces are designed to accommodate these needs. Accessibility features may include alternative text for images, captions for videos, keyboard navigation, color contrast, and clear, concise content. By incorporating accessibility into design processes, products become more inclusive, allowing a wider range of users to engage with and benefit from digital experiences, ultimately fostering equality and equal access for all. in Design Systems Adobe Spectrum Alaska Airlines Auro Amazon Cloudscape Apple HIG Atlassian Design System Carrefour Marcel Cisco Momentum Design Dell eBay Evo Elastic Esri Calcite General Electric Edison Github Primer GitLab Pajamas Google Material Design GOV.UK Gusto Workbench Helly Hansen IBM Carbon Ideagen Helix INPS Sirio Kiwicom Orbit LocalTapiola Duet Michelin Microsoft Fluent 2 Monday.com Vibe MongoDB LeafyGreen Nordhealth Orange Palantir Blueprint Pinterest Gestalt Porsche Red Hat PatternFly Rei Cedar Salesforce Lightning Samsung One UI ServiceNow Horizon Shopify Polaris Skoda Flow Teamleader Ahoy Thumbtack Thumbprint Twilio Paste UAE Uber Base Visa Washington Post Wikimedia Codex Wise Wonderflow Wanda Workday Canvas WorkOS Radix
